description: Initial execution result for task task6

data.task_objective: Implement the Final Report Generation Module: assemble resume-job match data, interview summaries, technical and behavioral assessments, strengths/weaknesses, and recommendations into a structured report.
data.context:
Task task0:
  Objective: Define the database schema for job descriptions, candidate resumes, interview sessions, AI-generated questions, candidate responses, evaluation scores, email logs, and final reports.
  Result: ```json
{
  "schemas": {
    "job_descriptions": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "job_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the job description",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "title": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job title"
        },
        "description": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Full job description text"
        },
        "requirements": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job requirements"
        },
        "responsibilities": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job responsibilities"
        },
        "department": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Department the job belongs to"
        },
        "location": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job location"
        },
        "date_posted": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date",
          "description": "Date the job was posted"
        },
        "embedding": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "number"
          },
          "description": "Vector embedding of the job description"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "job_id",
        "title",
        "description"
      ]
    },
    "candidate_resumes": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the candidate",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "name": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's full name"
        },
        "email": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's email address"
        },
        "phone": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's phone number"
        },
        "resume_text": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Full text of the candidate's resume"
        },
        "skills": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "description": "List of skills extracted from the resume"
        },
        "experience": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's work experience"
        },
        "education": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's education details"
        },
        "embedding": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "number"
          },
          "description": "Vector embedding of the resume"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "candidate_id",
        "name",
        "email",
        "resume_text"
      ]
    },
    "interview_sessions": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the interview session",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing candidate_resumes"
        },
        "job_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing job_descriptions"
        },
        "start_time": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the interview started"
        },
        "end_time": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the interview ended"
        },
        "status": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "scheduled",
            "in_progress",
            "completed",
            "cancelled"
          ],
          "description": "Status of the interview session"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "session_id",
        "candidate_id",
        "job_id",
        "start_time"
      ]
    },
    "ai_generated_questions": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "question_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the question",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
        "question_text": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "The text of the question generated by AI"
        },
        "question_type": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "technical",
            "behavioral",
            "follow_up"
          ],
          "description": "Type of question"
        },
        "expected_answer": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "The expected answer or keywords for the question"
        },
         "topic": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Topic of the question"
        },
        "difficulty": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["easy", "medium", "hard"],
          "description": "Difficulty level of the question"
         }
      },
      "required": [
        "question_id",
        "session_id",
        "question_text",
        "question_type"
      ]
    },
    "candidate_responses": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "response_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the response",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
        "question_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing ai_generated_questions"
        },
        "response_text": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "The candidate's answer to the question"
        },
        "timestamp": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the response was recorded"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "response_id",
        "session_id",
        "question_id",
        "response_text"
      ]
    },
    "evaluation_scores": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "score_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the score",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
         "question_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing ai_generated_questions"
        },
        "category": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "relevance",
            "depth",
            "confidence",
            "technical_accuracy",
            "communication_skills",
            "problem_solving"
          ],
          "description": "Category being evaluated"
        },
        "score": {
          "type": "number",
          "description": "Score for the category (e.g., 1-5 scale)"
        },
        "justification": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Explanation or justification for the score"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "score_id",
        "session_id",
        "category",
        "score"
      ]
    },
    "email_logs": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "email_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the email",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing candidate_resumes"
        },
        "email_type": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "invitation",
            "follow_up",
            "rejection",
            "offer"
          ],
          "description": "Type of email"
        },
        "subject": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Email subject"
        },
        "body": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Email body text"
        },
        "timestamp": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the email was sent"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "email_id",
        "candidate_id",
        "email_type",
        "subject",
        "body",
        "timestamp"
      ]
    },
    "final_reports": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "report_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the final report",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing candidate_resumes"
        },
        "job_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing job_descriptions"
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
        "resume_job_match_analysis": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Analysis of how well the resume matches the job description"
        },
        "interview_summary": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Summary of the interview session"
        },
        "technical_assessment": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Assessment of the candidate's technical skills"
        },
        "behavioral_assessment": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Assessment of the candidate's behavioral traits"
        },
        "strengths": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "List of the candidate's strengths"
        },
        "weaknesses": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "List of the candidate's weaknesses"
        },
        "final_recommendation": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Final recommendation (e.g., hire, no hire, further review)"
        },
        "overall_score": {
          "type": "number",
          "description": "Overall score for the candidate"
        },
        "scoring_breakdown": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Detailed breakdown of the scoring across different categories (JSON format)"
        },
         "report_generation_date": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the report was generated"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "report_id",
        "candidate_id",
        "job_id",
        "session_id"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```
Task task1:
  Objective: Design the RESTful API specification (endpoints, request/response models) for core operations: ingesting jobs/resumes, orchestrating interviews, submitting responses, scoring, emailing, and retrieving reports.
  Result: ```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: AI-Driven Interview and Candidate Evaluation System API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: RESTful API for managing jobs, resumes, interviews, evaluations, and reports.

paths:
  /jobs:
    post:
      summary: Ingest a new job description
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                job_title:
                  type: string
                  description: Title of the job
                job_description:
                  type: string
                  description: Detailed description of the job
                department:
                  type: string
                  description: Department for the job
                location:
                  type: string
                  description: Job location
                skills:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: List of required skills
              example:
                job_title: Software Engineer
                job_description: Develop and maintain software applications.  Experience with Python and Javascript required.
                department: Engineering
                location: New York, NY
                skills: [Python, Javascript, React, AWS]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Job created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  job_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the job
                example:
                  job_id: job123
        '400':
          description: Invalid request

    get:
      summary: Retrieve a list of all jobs
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of jobs retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
                  properties:
                    job_id:
                      type: string
                      description: Unique identifier for the job
                    job_title:
                      type: string
                      description: Title of the job
                    job_description:
                      type: string
                      description: Detailed description of the job
                    department:
                      type: string
                      description: Department for the job
                    location:
                      type: string
                      description: Job location
                    skills:
                      type: array
                      items:
                        type: string
                      description: List of required skills
                  example:
                    job_id: job123
                    job_title: Software Engineer
                    job_description: Develop and maintain software applications.  Experience with Python and Javascript required.
                    department: Engineering
                    location: New York, NY
                    skills: [Python, Javascript, React, AWS]

  /jobs/{job_id}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a specific job by ID
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: job_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the job
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Job retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  job_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the job
                  job_title:
                    type: string
                    description: Title of the job
                  job_description:
                    type: string
                    description: Detailed description of the job
                  department:
                    type: string
                    description: Department for the job
                  location:
                    type: string
                    description: Job location
                  skills:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of required skills
                example:
                  job_id: job123
                  job_title: Software Engineer
                  job_description: Develop and maintain software applications.  Experience with Python and Javascript required.
                  department: Engineering
                  location: New York, NY
                  skills: [Python, Javascript, React, AWS]
        '404':
          description: Job not found

  /resumes:
    post:
      summary: Ingest a new candidate resume
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                candidate_name:
                  type: string
                  description: Name of the candidate
                resume_text:
                  type: string
                  description: Text content of the resume
                email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  description: Candidate's email address
                skills:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: List of skills extracted from the resume
              example:
                candidate_name: John Doe
                resume_text: Experienced software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.
                email: john.doe@example.com
                skills: [Python, Javascript, SQL]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Resume created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  resume_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the resume
                example:
                  resume_id: resume456
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a list of all resumes
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of resumes retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
                  properties:
                    resume_id:
                      type: string
                      description: Unique identifier for the resume
                    candidate_name:
                      type: string
                      description: Name of the candidate
                    resume_text:
                      type: string
                      description: Text content of the resume
                    email:
                      type: string
                      format: email
                      description: Candidate's email address
                    skills:
                      type: array
                      items:
                        type: string
                      description: List of skills extracted from the resume
                  example:
                    resume_id: resume456
                    candidate_name: John Doe
                    resume_text: Experienced software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.
                    email: john.doe@example.com
                    skills: [Python, Javascript, SQL]

  /resumes/{resume_id}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a specific resume by ID
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: resume_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the resume
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Resume retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  resume_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the resume
                  candidate_name:
                    type: string
                    description: Name of the candidate
                  resume_text:
                    type: string
                    description: Text content of the resume
                  email:
                    type: string
                    format: email
                    description: Candidate's email address
                  skills:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of skills extracted from the resume
                example:
                  resume_id: resume456
                  candidate_name: John Doe
                  resume_text: Experienced software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.
                  email: john.doe@example.com
                  skills: [Python, Javascript, SQL]
        '404':
          description: Resume not found

  /interviews:
    post:
      summary: Start a new interview session
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                job_id:
                  type: string
                  description: ID of the job for the interview
                resume_id:
                  type: string
                  description: ID of the candidate's resume
              example:
                job_id: job123
                resume_id: resume456
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Interview session started successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  interview_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the interview session
                  first_question:
                    type: string
                    description: The first question to ask the candidate
                example:
                  interview_id: interview789
                  first_question: "Tell me about your experience with Python."
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '404':
          description: Job or Resume not found

  /interviews/{interview_id}/questions:
    get:
      summary: Get the current or next question in the interview
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Question retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  question:
                    type: string
                    description: The question to ask the candidate
                example:
                  question: "Explain the difference between '==' and '===' in Javascript."
        '404':
          description: Interview not found

  /interviews/{interview_id}/responses:
    post:
      summary: Submit a candidate's response to a question
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                question:
                  type: string
                  description: The question being answered.
                response:
                  type: string
                  description: The candidate's answer to the question
              example:
                question: "Explain the difference between '==' and '===' in Javascript."
                response: "'==' compares values, while '===' compares values and types."
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Response submitted successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  next_question:
                    type: string
                    nullable: true
                    description: The next question to ask, or null if the interview is complete
                  evaluation:
                    type: object
                    description: Evaluation of the response
                    properties:
                      relevance:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Relevance score (0-1)
                      depth:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Depth score (0-1)
                      confidence:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Confidence score (0-1)
                      technical_accuracy:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Technical accuracy score (0-1)
                    example:
                      relevance: 0.9
                      depth: 0.8
                      confidence: 0.7
                      technical_accuracy: 0.95
                example:
                  next_question: "How would you optimize a slow SQL query?"
                  evaluation:
                    relevance: 0.9
                    depth: 0.8
                    confidence: 0.7
                    technical_accuracy: 0.95
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '404':
          description: Interview not found

  /interviews/{interview_id}/scores:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve the candidate's scores for the interview
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Scores retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  technical_score:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                    description: Overall technical score
                  behavioral_score:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                    description: Overall behavioral score
                  overall_score:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                    description: Overall score
                  category_scores:
                    type: object
                    description: Scores for individual categories
                    properties:
                      problem_solving:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Problem-solving score
                      communication:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Communication score
                    example:
                      problem_solving: 0.85
                      communication: 0.9
                example:
                  technical_score: 0.8
                  behavioral_score: 0.9
                  overall_score: 0.85
                  category_scores:
                    problem_solving: 0.85
                    communication: 0.9
        '404':
          description: Interview not found

  /emails/invite:
    post:
      summary: Generate and send an interview invitation email
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                candidate_email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  description: Candidate's email address
                job_title:
                  type: string
                  description: Job title
                interview_date:
                  type: string
                  format: date-time
                  description: Date and time of the interview
                interviewer_name:
                  type: string
                  description: Name of the interviewer
              example:
                candidate_email: john.doe@example.com
                job_title: Software Engineer
                interview_date: "2024-01-20T10:00:00Z"
                interviewer_name: Jane Smith
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Email sent successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    description: Confirmation message
                example:
                  message: "Interview invitation email sent to john.doe@example.com"
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '500':
          description: Email sending failed

  /emails/followup:
    post:
      summary: Generate and send a follow-up email to the candidate
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                candidate_email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  description: Candidate's email address
                interview_id:
                  type: string
                  description: Interview ID
              example:
                candidate_email: john.doe@example.com
                interview_id: interview789
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Email sent successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    description: Confirmation message
                example:
                  message: "Follow-up email sent to john.doe@example.com"
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '500':
          description: Email sending failed

  /reports/{interview_id}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve the final candidate evaluation report
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Report retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  resume_job_match_analysis:
                    type: string
                    description: Analysis of how well the resume matches the job description
                  interview_summary:
                    type: string
                    description: Summary of the interview
                  technical_assessment:
                    type: string
                    description: Assessment of the candidate's technical skills
                  behavioral_assessment:
                    type: string
                    description: Assessment of the candidate's behavioral traits
                  strengths:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of the candidate's strengths
                  weaknesses:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of the candidate's weaknesses
                  final_recommendation:
                    type: string
                    description: Final recommendation (Hire/No Hire/Maybe)
                  scoring_breakdown:
                    type: object
                    description: Detailed breakdown of the candidate's scores
                    properties:
                      technical_score:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Overall technical score
                      behavioral_score:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Overall behavioral score
                      overall_score:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Overall score
                    example:
                      technical_score: 0.8
                      behavioral_score: 0.9
                      overall_score: 0.85
                example:
                  resume_job_match_analysis: "The candidate's resume closely matches the job description, with strong skills in Python and Javascript."
                  interview_summary: "The candidate performed well in the interview, demonstrating strong technical knowledge and communication skills."
                  technical_assessment: "The candidate has a solid understanding of software engineering principles and is proficient in Python and Javascript."
                  behavioral_assessment: "The candidate is a good communicator and works well in a team environment."
                  strengths: ["Technical Skills", "Communication", "Problem Solving"]
                  weaknesses: ["Lack of experience with AWS"]
                  final_recommendation: "Hire"
                  scoring_breakdown:
                    technical_score: 0.8
                    behavioral_score: 0.9
                    overall_score: 0.85
        '404':
          description: Report not found
components:
  schemas:
    Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        message:
          type: string
          description: Error message
      example:
        message: "An error occurred"

Task task2:
  Objective: Implement the Resume–Job Matching Module: parse and embed job descriptions and resumes, compute semantic similarity scores, and expose matching interface.
  Result: ```python
import nltk
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
from nltk.corpus import stopwords
from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize
import re
from typing import List, Tuple

# Download required NLTK data (if not already downloaded)
try:
    nltk.data.find('corpora/stopwords')
except LookupError:
    nltk.download('stopwords')

try:
    nltk.data.find('tokenizers/punkt')
except LookupError:
    nltk.download('punkt')


class ResumeJobMatcher:
    """
    A class for matching resumes to job descriptions based on semantic similarity.
    """

    def __init__(self, embedding_model=None):
        """
        Initializes the ResumeJobMatcher with an optional embedding model.

        Args:
            embedding_model:  A pre-trained word embedding model (e.g., Word2Vec, GloVe).
                              If None, a simple TF-IDF based approach is used.  Must have a 'encode' method.
        """
        self.embedding_model = embedding_model
        self.stop_words = set(stopwords.words('english'))

    def preprocess_text(self, text: str) -> str:
        """
        Preprocesses the input text by removing special characters,
        converting to lowercase, and removing stop words.

        Args:
            text (str): The input text to preprocess.

        Returns:
            str: The preprocessed text.
        """
        text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', '', text)
        text = text.lower()
        tokens = word_tokenize(text)
        tokens = [w for w in tokens if not w in self.stop_words]
        return " ".join(tokens)

    def compute_similarity(self, embedding1: np.ndarray, embedding2: np.ndarray) -> float:
        """
        Computes the cosine similarity between two embeddings.

        Args:
            embedding1 (np.ndarray): The first embedding.
            embedding2 (np.ndarray): The second embedding.

        Returns:
            float: The cosine similarity score.
        """
        if np.linalg.norm(embedding1) == 0 or np.linalg.norm(embedding2) == 0:
            return 0.0  # Handle zero vector case
        return cosine_similarity(embedding1.reshape(1, -1), embedding2.reshape(1, -1))[0][0]

    def match(self, job_description: str, resume_text: str) -> float:
        """
        Matches a job description to a resume based on semantic similarity
        of their embeddings.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description text.
            resume_text (str): The resume text.

        Returns:
            float: The similarity score between the job description and resume.
        """
        preprocessed_job_description = self.preprocess_text(job_description)
        preprocessed_resume_text = self.preprocess_text(resume_text)

        if self.embedding_model:
            job_embedding = self.embedding_model.encode(preprocessed_job_description)
            resume_embedding = self.embedding_model.encode(preprocessed_resume_text)
        else:
            raise ValueError("Embedding model not provided")


        similarity_score = self.compute_similarity(job_embedding, resume_embedding)
        return similarity_score

    def extract_skills(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
        """
        Extracts skills from the given text (resume or job description).  This is a placeholder;
        a real implementation would use NLP techniques or a skills database.

        Args:
            text (str): The text to extract skills from.

        Returns:
            List[str]: A list of skills found in the text.
        """
        # Placeholder implementation: split the text into words and return unique words
        # In a real implementation, use NLP techniques to identify skills
        words = self.preprocess_text(text).split()
        return list(set(words))


    def analyze_match(self, job_description: str, resume_text: str) -> Tuple[float, List[str], List[str]]:
        """
        Analyzes the match between a job description and a resume, returning the similarity score,
        skills found in the job description, and skills found in the resume.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description text.
            resume_text (str): The resume text.

        Returns:
            Tuple[float, List[str], List[str]]: A tuple containing the similarity score,
                                                 list of job description skills, and list of resume skills.
        """
        similarity_score = self.match(job_description, resume_text)
        job_skills = self.extract_skills(job_description)
        resume_skills = self.extract_skills(resume_text)
        return similarity_score, job_skills, resume_skills


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage (requires a dummy embedding model for testing)
    class DummyEmbeddingModel:
        def encode(self, text):
            # Replace with actual embedding logic in production
            return np.random.rand(100)  # Return a random vector as a placeholder

    job_description = "Software Engineer with experience in Python and Javascript"
    resume_text = "Experienced software engineer proficient in Python, Javascript, and SQL"

    embedding_model = DummyEmbeddingModel()
    matcher = ResumeJobMatcher(embedding_model=embedding_model)

    similarity_score, job_skills, resume_skills = matcher.analyze_match(job_description, resume_text)

    print(f"Similarity Score: {similarity_score}")
    print(f"Job Description Skills: {job_skills}")
    print(f"Resume Skills: {resume_skills}")
```
Task task4:
  Objective: Develop the Candidate Evaluation & Scoring Logic: analyze candidate responses for relevance, depth, confidence, and technical accuracy; compute composite scores across defined categories.
  Result: ```python
# candidate_evaluation.py

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, List
import numpy as np
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer, util
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
import logging
import json  # Import for handling JSON-formatted scoring breakdown
from enum import Enum

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class QuestionType(str, Enum):
    TECHNICAL = "technical"
    BEHAVIORAL = "behavioral"
    FOLLOW_UP = "follow_up"

@dataclass
class ScoringConfig:
    relevance_weight: float = 0.8
    depth_weight: float = 0.7
    confidence_weight: float = 0.6
    technical_accuracy_weight: float = 0.9

    # Composite weights
    technical_skills_weights: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "depth": 0.5,
        "technical_accuracy": 0.5
    })
    communication_skills_weights: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "relevance": 0.6,
        "confidence": 0.4
    })
    problem_solving_weights: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "relevance": 0.4,
        "depth": 0.3,
        "technical_accuracy": 0.3
    })
    overall_weights: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "technical_skills": 0.35,
        "communication_skills": 0.35,
        "problem_solving": 0.30
    })
    behavioral_weights: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "relevance": 0.5,
        "depth": 0.25,
        "confidence": 0.25
    })

    # Models
    embedding_model_name: str = 'all-MiniLM-L6-v2'
    confidence_lexicon: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "confident": 0.8, "certainly": 0.7, "definitely": 0.9, "sure": 0.6, "experience": 0.5,
        "I am confident": 0.9, "I know": 0.7, "I have experience": 0.8
    })
    hesitant_lexicon: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "I think": -0.3, "I believe": -0.2, "maybe": -0.5, "possibly": -0.4, "not sure": -0.6
    })

class CandidateEvaluator:
    def __init__(self, config: ScoringConfig):
        self.config = config
        try:
            self.embed_model = SentenceTransformer(config.embedding_model_name)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to load embedding model: {e}")
            raise
        self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer()

    def _semantic_similarity(self, texts1: List[str], texts2: List[str]) -> float:
        """Compute cosine similarity between two lists of texts using embeddings."""
        if not texts1 or not texts2:
            return 0.0
        try:
            emb1 = self.embed_model.encode(texts1, convert_to_tensor=True)
            emb2 = self.embed_model.encode(texts2, convert_to_tensor=True)
            sim = util.cos_sim(emb1, emb2).item()
            return float(np.clip(sim, 0.0, 1.0))
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Semantic similarity calculation failed: {e}")
            return 0.0

    def calculate_relevance_score(self, response: str, expected_answer: str, job_description: str) -> float:
        """Semantic similarity between response and combined context."""
        try:
            context = expected_answer + " " + job_description
            score = self._semantic_similarity([response], [context])
            return score * self.config.relevance_weight
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Relevance score calculation failed: {e}")
            return 0.0

    def calculate_depth_score(self, response: str, expected_answer: str) -> float:
        """Combines response length ratio and TF-IDF concept richness."""
        try:
            resp_len = len(response.split())
            exp_len = max(1, len(expected_answer.split()))
            length_ratio = min(resp_len / exp_len, 1.0)

            corpus = [expected_answer, response]
            tfidf = self.tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(corpus)
            tfidf_array = tfidf.toarray()

            richness = tfidf_array[1].sum() / (tfidf_array[0].sum() + 1e-6)
            richness_norm = min(richness, 1.0)

            raw = 0.5 * length_ratio + 0.5 * richness_norm
            depth_score = 1 / (1 + np.exp(-10 * (raw - 0.5)))
            return float(depth_score * self.config.depth_weight)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Depth score calculation failed: {e}")
            return 0.0

    def calculate_confidence_score(self, response: str) -> float:
        """Confidence via lexicon and sentiment polarity."""
        try:
            resp_lower = response.lower()
            lexical_score = 0.0
            for kw, weight in self.config.confidence_lexicon.items():
                if kw.lower() in resp_lower:
                    lexical_score += weight
            for kw, weight in self.config.hesitant_lexicon.items():
                if kw.lower() in resp_lower:
                    lexical_score += weight

            lexical_score = np.clip(lexical_score, -1.0, 1.0)
            lexical_score = (lexical_score + 1) / 2  # Normalize to 0-1 range

            dist = 1 - self._semantic_similarity([response], ["I am not confident"])
            sem_score = dist

            combined = 0.5 * lexical_score + 0.5 * sem_score
            return float(np.clip(combined * self.config.confidence_weight, 0.0, 1.0))
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Confidence score calculation failed: {e}")
            return 0.0

    def calculate_technical_accuracy_score(self, response: str, expected_answer: str) -> float:
        """Technical accuracy as semantic similarity."""
        try:
            score = self._semantic_similarity([response], [expected_answer])
            return float(score * self.config.technical_accuracy_weight)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Technical accuracy score calculation failed: {e}")
            return 0.0
    
    def calculate_behavioral_score(self, scores: Dict[str, float]) -> float:
        """Calculate overall behavioral score using configured weights."""
        try:
            behavioral_score = sum(scores[k] * w for k, w in self.config.behavioral_weights.items())
            return float(np.clip(behavioral_score, 0.0, 1.0))
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Behavioral score calculation failed: {e}")
            return 0.0

    def compute_composite_scores(self, scores: Dict[str, float]) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Build composite categories using configurable weights."""
        try:
            tech = sum(scores[k] * w for k, w in self.config.technical_skills_weights.items())
            comm = sum(scores[k] * w for k, w in self.config.communication_skills_weights.items())
            prob = sum(scores[k] * w for k, w in self.config.problem_solving_weights.items())

            technical_skills = float(np.clip(tech, 0.0, 1.0))
            communication_skills = float(np.clip(comm, 0.0, 1.0))
            problem_solving = float(np.clip(prob, 0.0, 1.0))

            overall = sum(val * self.config.overall_weights[key] for key, val in {
                "technical_skills": technical_skills,
                "communication_skills": communication_skills,
                "problem_solving": problem_solving
            }.items())

            return {
                "technical_skills": technical_skills,
                "communication_skills": communication_skills,
                "problem_solving": problem_solving,
                "overall_score": float(np.clip(overall, 0.0, 1.0))
            }
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Composite score calculation failed: {e}")
            return {
                "technical_skills": 0.0,
                "communication_skills": 0.0,
                "problem_solving": 0.0,
                "overall_score": 0.0
            }

    def evaluate(self, response: str, expected_answer: str, job_description: str, question_type: QuestionType = QuestionType.TECHNICAL) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Run full evaluation pipeline."""
        response = response or ""
        expected_answer = expected_answer or ""
        job_description = job_description or ""

        try:
            scores = {
                "relevance": self.calculate_relevance_score(response, expected_answer, job_description),
                "depth": self.calculate_depth_score(response, expected_answer),
                "confidence": self.calculate_confidence_score(response),
                "technical_accuracy": self.calculate_technical_accuracy_score(response, expected_answer)
            }

            composite_scores = self.compute_composite_scores(scores)

            # Include behavioral score if question type is behavioral
            if question_type == QuestionType.BEHAVIORAL:
                behavioral_score = self.calculate_behavioral_score(scores)
                composite_scores["behavioral_score"] = behavioral_score

            return {**scores, **composite_scores}
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Overall evaluation failed: {e}")
            return {
                "relevance": 0.0,
                "depth": 0.0,
                "confidence": 0.0,
                "technical_accuracy": 0.0,
                "technical_skills": 0.0,
                "communication_skills": 0.0,
                "problem_solving": 0.0,
                "overall_score": 0.0,
                "behavioral_score": 0.0 if question_type == QuestionType.BEHAVIORAL else None
            }

    def generate_scoring_breakdown_json(self, evaluation_results: Dict[str, float]) -> str:
        """
        Generates a JSON string representing the scoring breakdown.
        """
        try:
            return json.dumps(evaluation_results, indent=2)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to generate scoring breakdown JSON: {e}")
            return "{}"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    cfg = ScoringConfig()
    evaluator = CandidateEvaluator(cfg)
    resp = ("I have experience building microservices in Python, "
            "using Docker and Kubernetes. I'm confident in designing scalable APIs.")
    expected = ("Experience with Python, Docker, Kubernetes, and API design is required.")
    jd = ("We need a backend engineer skilled in Python, containerization, "
          "and scalable system architecture.")

    # Example Usage for technical question
    results_technical = evaluator.evaluate(resp, expected, jd, QuestionType.TECHNICAL)
    print("Technical Evaluation Results:", results_technical)
    scoring_breakdown_technical = evaluator.generate_scoring_breakdown_json(results_technical)
    print("Technical Scoring Breakdown JSON:\n", scoring_breakdown_technical)

    # Example Usage for behavioral question
    resp_behavioral = "I work well in teams and can effectively communicate technical concepts."
    expected_behavioral = "Demonstrates teamwork and communication skills."
    jd_behavioral = "Looking for team players with good communication abilities."
    results_behavioral = evaluator.evaluate(resp_behavioral, expected_behavioral, jd_behavioral, QuestionType.BEHAVIORAL)
    print("\nBehavioral Evaluation Results:", results_behavioral)
    scoring_breakdown_behavioral = evaluator.generate_scoring_breakdown_json(results_behavioral)
    print("Behavioral Scoring Breakdown JSON:\n", scoring_breakdown_behavioral)
```

```python
# final_report_generator.py

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import json
import logging
from datetime import datetime

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass
class ReportData:
    """
    A data class to hold all the information needed to generate a final report.
    """
    resume_job_match_analysis: str
    interview_summary: str
    technical_assessment: str
    behavioral_assessment: str
    strengths: List[str]
    weaknesses: List[str]
    final_recommendation: str
    overall_score: float
    scoring_breakdown: Dict[str, float]
    candidate_name: str
    job_title: str


class FinalReportGenerator:
    """
    A class for generating final candidate evaluation reports.
    """

    def generate_report(self, report_data: ReportData) -> str:
        """
        Generates a final candidate evaluation report as a formatted string.

        Args:
            report_data (ReportData): The data needed to generate the report.

        Returns:
            str: The final candidate evaluation report.
        """
        try:
            report = f"""
            Final Candidate Evaluation Report
            ===================================
            Report Generation Date: {datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")}

            Candidate: {report_data.candidate_name}
            Job Title: {report_data.job_title}

            1. Resume-Job Match Analysis:
               {report_data.resume_job_match_analysis}

            2. Interview Summary:
               {report_data.interview_summary}

            3. Technical Assessment:
               {report_data.technical_assessment}

            4. Behavioral Assessment:
               {report_data.behavioral_assessment}

            5. Strengths:
            """
            for strength in report_data.strengths:
                report += f"   - {strength}\n"

            report += """
            6. Weaknesses:
            """
            for weakness in report_data.weaknesses:
                report += f"   - {weakness}\n"

            report += f"""
            7. Final Recommendation:
               {report_data.final_recommendation}

            8. Overall Score:
               {report_data.overall_score:.2f}

            9. Scoring Breakdown:
               {json.dumps(report_data.scoring_breakdown, indent=4)}

            End of Report
            """

            return report

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Report generation failed: {e}")
            return "Error generating report."

    def save_report_to_file(self, report: str, filename: str) -> None:
        """
        Saves the generated report to a file.

        Args:
            report (str): The report content.
            filename (str): The name of the file to save the report to.
        """
        try:
            with open(filename, "w") as f:
                f.write(report)
            logger.info(f"Report saved to {filename}")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to save report to file: {e}")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    report_data = ReportData(
        resume_job_match_analysis="The candidate's resume closely matches the job description, with strong skills in Python and Javascript.",
        interview_summary="The candidate performed well in the interview, demonstrating strong technical knowledge and communication skills.",
        technical_assessment="The candidate has a solid understanding of software engineering principles and is proficient in Python and Javascript.",
        behavioral_assessment="The candidate is a good communicator and works well in a team environment.",
        strengths=["Technical Skills", "Communication", "Problem Solving"],
        weaknesses=["Lack of experience with AWS"],
        final_recommendation="Hire",
        overall_score=0.85,
        scoring_breakdown={
            "technical_score": 0.8,
            "behavioral_score": 0.9,
            "problem_solving": 0.85,
            "communication_skills": 0.9
        },
        candidate_name="John Doe",
        job_title="Software Engineer"
    )

    report_generator = FinalReportGenerator()
    report = report_generator.generate_report(report_data)
    print(report)

    report_generator.save_report_to_file(report, "candidate_evaluation_report.txt")
```
